title: Amauropelta oppositiformis
intro: Amauropelta oppositiformis, a species of fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The health benefits of Amauropelta oppositiformis include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Amauropelta oppositiformis is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies, such as tonics and salves, often to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Amauropelta oppositiformis belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a family of ferns. It is classified under the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. Further classification places it under the division Pteridophyta and the kingdom Plantae. This plant is native to the Andes mountains. It is a species of fern that is endemic to this region.
